title: Turnstile World

mood.label: Uneasy anticipation
mood.summary: The World Cup looms five days out but war and visa walls keep fans away, Ebola nears 400 confirmed cases in Congo, the Senate passes an immigration bill at dawn, California picks its next governor, and Lebanon's president calls the ceasefire a last chance — the world spinning between spectacle and crisis.

sampleSources: Strudel default library — sampled piano, vibraphone (VCSL), RolandTR808

lines: Five days to kickoff and the turnstiles wait,
but the visa line wraps around a war nobody sees on screen.
A senator signs at dawn, the border bill is late,
and California picks a name to fill the in-between.
Three hundred eighty-one in a ward in Ituri,
the outbreak learns the roads before the doctors do.
In Beirut a president says this chance won't come again —
last chance, last ceasefire, last line to push through.
Turnstile world, spinning us between the match and the ward,
the stadium lights and the radar in the strait.
We buy our tickets, we brace for what we can't afford —
uneasy anticipation, and the game won't wait.
